devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । बलभद्रः कुरुश्रेष्ठ भगवान् रथमास्थितः । सुहृद्दिदृक्षुरुत्कण्ठः प्रययौ नन्दगोकुलम् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 001 ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācabalabhadraḥ kuruśreṣṭha bhagavān rathamāsthitaḥsuhṛddidṛkṣurutkaṇṭhaḥ prayayau nandagokulam || 10 . 65 .
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
(On one occasion) the glorious Lord Balarāma became eagerly desirous of seeing his friends and well-wishers (at Vraja). Mounting his chariot, he proceeded[1] to Gokula ruled over by Nanda, O the most prominent Kuru.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Pra-yayau—went after wearing the dress of a Gopa (cowherd)—VT. It quotes HV. in its support and informs us that Balarāma went to convey the massage of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: परिष्वक्तश्चिरोत्कण्ठैर्गोपैर्गोपीभिरेव च । रामोऽभिवाद्य पितरावाशीर्भिरभिनन्दितः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 002 ॥
english_devnagari: pariṣvaktaścirotkaṇṭhairgopairgopībhireva carāmo'bhivādya pitarāvāśīrbhirabhinanditaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Balarāma was warmly embraced by Gopa men and women (at Vraja) who were yearning (to see him) for a pretty long period. He bowed to his (foster) parents Nanda and Yaśodā who welcomed him with blessings (and said):

devanagari: चिरं नः पाहि दाशार्ह सानुजो जगदीश्वरः । इत्यारोप्याङ्कमालिङ्ग्य नेत्रैः सिषिचतुर्जलैः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 003 ॥
english_devnagari: ciraṃ naḥ pāhi dāśārha sānujo jagadīśvaraḥityāropyāṅkamāliṅgya netraiḥ siṣicaturjalaiḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: “O Balarāma (of the Dāśārha clan)! May you, the Lord of the universe along with your younger brother Kṛṣṇa, protect us for a long time (just as you do to Yādavas)”. With these words, they placed him on their lap, hugged him and bathed him with tears.

devanagari: गोपवृद्धांश्च विधिवद्यविष्ठैरभिवन्दितः । यथावयो यथासख्यं यथासम्बन्धमात्मनः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 004 ॥
english_devnagari: gopavṛddhāṃśca vidhivadyaviṣṭhairabhivanditaḥyathāvayo yathāsakhyaṃ yathāsambandhamātmanaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: He paid respects to elderly Gopas with due formalities and was bowed down by his juniors in age. He met other Gopas (observing courteous exchanges) according to their age, friendly intimacy and according to their (respective) relations (with him).

devanagari: समुपेत्याथ गोपालान् हास्यहस्तग्रहादिभिः । विश्रान्तं सुखमासीनं पप्रच्छुः पर्युपागताः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 005 ॥
english_devnagari: samupetyātha gopālān hāsyahastagrahādibhiḥviśrāntaṃ sukhamāsīnaṃ papracchuḥ paryupāgatāḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: He then approached the cowherds,[2] cut jokes and laughed with them, held them by hands, (expressed other friendly gestures). When he had taken rest and was refreshed, they gathered and seated themselves round him as he was comfortably seated.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
VT. carefully collects evidence of the visits of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa from Viṣṇu Purāṇa & HV. and points out that they used to visit at intervals when they happened to come to that side.

devanagari: पृष्टाश्चानामयं स्वेषु प्रेमगद्गदया गिरा । कृष्णे कमलपत्राक्षे सन्न्यस्ताखिलराधसः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 006 ॥
english_devnagari: pṛṣṭāścānāmayaṃ sveṣu premagadgadayā girākṛṣṇe kamalapatrākṣe sannyastākhilarādhasaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: The Gopas who had renounced their worldly pleasures for Śrī Kṛṣṇa of lotus—(petal-like) eyes were enquired about their health and of their relatives in a voice choked with emotions (and in faltering accents). They also reciprocally enquired about the health of Yādavas as per their relations).

devanagari: कच्चिन्नो बान्धवा राम सर्वे कुशलमासते । कच्चित्स्मरथ नो राम यूयं दारसुतान्विताः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 007 ॥
english_devnagari: kaccinno bāndhavā rāma sarve kuśalamāsatekaccitsmaratha no rāma yūyaṃ dārasutānvitāḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: “Are all our friends and relatives (at Dvārakā) hail and hearty, O Balarāma? Now that you are a married person blessed with children, do you (occasionally) remember us?

devanagari: दिष्ट्या कंसो हतः पापो दिष्ट्या मुक्ताः सुहृज्जनाः । निहत्य निर्जित्य रिपून् दिष्ट्या दुर्गं समाश्रीताः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 008 ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā kaṃso hataḥ pāpo diṣṭyā muktāḥ suhṛjjanāḥnihatya nirjitya ripūn diṣṭyā durgaṃ samāśrītāḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Luckily the wicked Kaṃsa has been slain and it is fortunate that all our friends and well-wishers are liberated (from his atrocious rule). It is really a matter of good luck that after killing and vanquishing your enemies, you have taken your abode in an inaccessible place (Dvārakā)”.

devanagari: गोप्यो हसन्त्यः पप्रच्छू रामसन्दर्शनादृताः । कच्चिदास्ते सुखं कृष्णः पुरस्त्रीजनवल्लभः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 009 ॥
english_devnagari: gopyo hasantyaḥ papracchū rāmasandarśanādṛtāḥkaccidāste sukhaṃ kṛṣṇaḥ purastrījanavallabhaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Gopīs looked upon Balarāma’s visit as an honour and paid respect to him (In spite of their pangs of separation from Kṛṣna) they beamed with a smile and asked, “Is Kṛṣṇa, the darling of ladies in the city (as against us—rustic women), happy?

devanagari: कच्चित्स्मरति वा बन्धून् पितरं मातरं च सः । अप्यसौ मातरं द्रष्टुं सकृदप्यागमिष्यति । अपि वा स्मरतेऽस्माकमनुसेवां महाभुजः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ ...
english_devnagari: kaccitsmarati vā bandhūn pitaraṃ mātaraṃ ca saḥapyasau mātaraṃ draṣṭuṃ sakṛdapyāgamiṣyatiapi vā smarate'smākamanusevāṃ mahābhujaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Does he occasionally remember his friends, (like the Gopa-Dāman and others), father (Nanda) and mother (Yaśoḍā) at Vraja? Will he come at least once to see his mother (Yaśoḍā)? Does that mighty-armed Lord ever remember our modest service (or constant devotion to him)?

devanagari: मातरं पितरं भ्रातॄन् पतीन् पुत्रान् स्वसॄनपि । यदर्थे जहिम दाशार्ह दुस्त्यजान् स्वजनान् प्रभो ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 011 ॥
english_devnagari: mātaraṃ pitaraṃ bhrātṝn patīn putrān svasṝnapiyadarthe jahima dāśārha dustyajān svajanān prabho || 10 . 65 .
translation: It is for his sake, O Balarāma that we abandoned our parents, brothers, husbands, sons, sisters and relatives whom it was hard to forsake.

devanagari: ता नः सद्यः परित्यज्य गतः सञ्छिन्नसौहृदः । कथं नु तादृशं स्त्रीभिर्न श्रद्धीयेत भाषितम् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 012 ॥
english_devnagari: tā naḥ sadyaḥ parityajya gataḥ sañchinnasauhṛdaḥkathaṃ nu tādṛśaṃ strībhirna śraddhīyeta bhāṣitam || 10 . 65 .
translation: Instantly he cut asunder the bonds of friendship with us who were so beloved to him and he abandoned us forthwith. (The cause of our not preventing him from going was his fascinatingly sweet) words—what woman would hesitate to put forth faith in them?

devanagari: कथं नु गृह्णन्त्यनवस्थितात्मनो वचः कृतघ्नस्य बुधाः पुरस्त्रियः । गृह्णन्ति वै चित्रकथस्य सुन्दर स्मितावलोकोच्छ्वसितस्मरातुराः ॥ 10. 6...
english_devnagari: kathaṃ nu gṛhṇantyanavasthitātmano vacaḥ kṛtaghnasya budhāḥ purastriyaḥgṛhṇanti vai citrakathasya sundara smitāvalokocchvasitasmarāturāḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: (Some Gopīs asked): How would the wise and shrewd women of the town trust the words of that fickle-minded and ungrateful person? (Others replied:) When he provokes the passion of love in them by his fascinating words, his winning smiles and enticing looks, those women being overpowered with love, put faith in his wonderful speech.

devanagari: किं नस्तत्कथया गोप्यः कथाः कथयतापराः । यात्यस्माभिर्विना कालो यदि तस्य तथैव नः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 014 ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ nastatkathayā gopyaḥ kathāḥ kathayatāparāḥyātyasmābhirvinā kālo yadi tasya tathaiva naḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: (Some said): O Gopīs! What have we to do with the talk about him? Tell some other stories. (Drop all references to Kṛṣṇa). If his time can pass (happily) without us, our time (reciprocally) can do so without him (though in pangs of separation from him)”.

devanagari: इति प्रहसितं शौरेर्जल्पितं चारुवीक्षितम् । गतिं प्रेमपरिष्वङ्गं स्मरन्त्यो रुरुदुः स्त्रियः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 015 ॥
english_devnagari: iti prahasitaṃ śaurerjalpitaṃ cāruvīkṣitamgatiṃ premapariṣvaṅgaṃ smarantyo ruruduḥ striyaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Recalling in this way the smiles, sweet words, winsome looks, gait, loving embraces of Kṛṣṇa, those Gopa women wept bitterly.

devanagari: सङ्कर्षणस्ताः कृष्णस्य सन्देशैर्हृदयंगमैः । सान्त्वयामास भगवान्नानानुनयकोविदः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 016 ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkarṣaṇastāḥ kṛṣṇasya sandeśairhṛdayaṃgamaiḥsāntvayāmāsa bhagavānnānānunayakovidaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: The worshipful Lord Balarāma, past-master in the various ways of consoling, comforted them by conveying to them massages of Śrī Kṛṣṇa which were most soothing to their heart.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
sandeśaiḥ sāma-madhuraiḥ premagarbhairagarvitaiḥ /
rāmeṇāśvāsitā gopyaḥ kṛṣṇasyātimanoharaiḥ //
  —Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5,24.20.

devanagari: द्वौ मासौ तत्र चावात्सीन्मधुं माधवं एव च । रामः क्षपासु भगवान् गोपीनां रतिमावहन् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 017 ॥
english_devnagari: dvau māsau tatra cāvātsīnmadhuṃ mādhavaṃ eva carāmaḥ kṣapāsu bhagavān gopīnāṃ ratimāvahan || 10 . 65 .
translation: The glorious Lord Balarāma spent there (in Gokula) two months (of the spring season)—Caitra and Vaiśākha and entertained the Gopīs at night.

devanagari: पूर्णचन्द्रकलामृष्टे कौमुदीगन्धवायुना । यमुनोपवने रेमे सेविते स्त्रीगणैर्वृतः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 018 ॥
english_devnagari: pūrṇacandrakalāmṛṣṭe kaumudīgandhavāyunāyamunopavane reme sevite strīgaṇairvṛtaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Surrounded by a bevy of ladies (Gopīs), Balarāma sported with them in the gardens on the bank of the Yamunā brightly illumined by the rays of the full moon as the gentle breeze charged with the fragrance of night lotuses was blowing.

devanagari: वरुणप्रेषिता देवी वारुणी वृक्षकोटरात् । पतन्ती तद्वनं सर्वं स्वगन्धेनाध्यवासयत् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 019 ॥
english_devnagari: varuṇapreṣitā devī vāruṇī vṛkṣakoṭarātpatantī tadvanaṃ sarvaṃ svagandhenādhyavāsayat || 10 . 65 .
translation: The heavenly beverage called Vāruṇī[4], sent by god Varuṇa began to flow out from the hallow of a tree and filled with its fragrance the whole of the forest.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Subodhinī Vāruṇī, born while chṇrning the ocean for nectar, was given to demons. Whatever tree she occupies gives out alcoholic beverage. VT. on the authority of HV. says that Vāruṇī was Varuṇa’s daughter and hence not forbidden. Likewise she creates the beverage in the hollow of the Kadamba tree.

devanagari: तं गन्धं मधुधाराया वायुनोपहृतं बलः । आघ्रायोपगतस्तत्र ललनाभिः समं पपौ ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 020 ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ gandhaṃ madhudhārāyā vāyunopahṛtaṃ balaḥāghrāyopagatastatra lalanābhiḥ samaṃ papau || 10 . 65 .
translation:
Inhaling the fragrance of that stream of honey (like beverage) wafted by the gentle wind, Balarāma went to that tree and drank it along with the women.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:]
20(A). Glorified in songs and himself bursting out in singing in the circle of beautiful women, he sported himself like Airāvata (Indra’s elephant) in company of she-elephants.
20(B). Kettle-drums were sounded in the heavens, the heavenly beings showered flowers on them in delight. Gandharvas and sages glorified Balarāma’s exploits in great joy.

devanagari: उपगीयमानो गन्धर्वैर्वनिताशोभिमण्डले । रेमे करेणुयूथेशो माहेन्द्र इव वारणः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 021 ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamāno gandharvairvanitāśobhimaṇḍalereme kareṇuyūtheśo māhendra iva vāraṇaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Balarāma (the plough-armed) whose exploits were glorified in songs by the Gopīs, roved in an inebriated condition in the forest, with his eyes swimming in intoxication.

devanagari: नेदुर्दुन्दुभयो व्योम्नि ववृषुः कुसुमैर्मुदा । गन्धर्वा मुनयो रामं तद्वीर्यैरीडिरे तदा ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 022 ॥
english_devnagari: nedurdundubhayo vyomni vavṛṣuḥ kusumairmudāgandharvā munayo rāmaṃ tadvīryairīḍire tadā || 10 . 65 .
translation: Adorned with a wreath of flowers and the special garland of five-coloured flowers called Vaijayantī, wearing one ear-ring only, with a smiling lotus-face beautified with dewy drops of perspiration, in an inebriated condition he commanded the Yamunā to come near him to enable him to indulge in water-sports. Being intoxicated, he thought that the Yamunā insolently disobeyed his order. Balarāma got enraged and forcibly dragged her with the pointed plough-share, of his plough.

devanagari: उपगीयमानचरितो वनिताभिर्हलायुध । वनेषु व्यचरत्क्षीवो मदविह्वललोचनः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 023 ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamānacarito vanitābhirhalāyudhavaneṣu vyacaratkṣīvo madavihvalalocanaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Adorned with a wreath of flowers and the special garland of five-coloured flowers called Vaijayantī, wearing one ear-ring only, with a smiling lotus-face beautified with dewy drops of perspiration, in an inebriated condition he commanded the Yamunā to come near him to enable him to indulge in water-sports. Being intoxicated, he thought that the Yamunā insolently disobeyed his order. Balarāma got enraged and forcibly dragged her with the pointed plough-share, of his plough.

devanagari: स्रग्व्येककुण्डलो मत्तो वैजयन्त्या च मालया । बिभ्रत्स्मितमुखाम्भोजं स्वेदप्रालेयभूषितम् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 024 ॥
english_devnagari: sragvyekakuṇḍalo matto vaijayantyā ca mālayābibhratsmitamukhāmbhojaṃ svedaprāleyabhūṣitam || 10 . 65 .
translation: (He threatened her): “You sinful one! In total disregard for me you indifferently do not come despite my summons to you, but go your own way. By the tip of my plough I shall now divert you in a hundred ways”.

devanagari: स आजुहाव यमुनां जलक्रीडार्थमीश्वरः । निजं वाक्यमनादृत्य मत्त इत्यापगां बलः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 025 ॥
english_devnagari: sa ājuhāva yamunāṃ jalakrīḍārthamīśvaraḥnijaṃ vākyamanādṛtya matta ityāpagāṃ balaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation: When threatened thus, the river-goddess Yamunā became afraid and fell at his feet. Trembling with fear she spoke to the delight of the Yadus.

devanagari: अनागतां हलाग्रेण कुपितो विचकर्ष ह । पापे त्वं मामवज्ञाय यन्नायासि मयाहुता । नेष्ये त्वां लाङ्गलाग्रेण शतधा कामचारिणीम् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 02...
english_devnagari: anāgatāṃ halāgreṇa kupito vicakarṣa hapāpe tvaṃ māmavajñāya yannāyāsi mayāhutāneṣye tvāṃ lāṅgalāgreṇa śatadhā kāmacāriṇīm || 10 . 65 .
translation: “O Balarāma, the Delight of the world! O mighty-armed hero! I did not know your prowess. O Lord of the world, (I now know) that it is with your one aṃśa (part) only, viz. Śeṣa that you support the universe.

devanagari: एवं निर्भर्त्सिता भीता यमुना यदुनन्दनम् । उवाच चकिता वाचं पतिता पादयोर्नृप ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 027 ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ nirbhartsitā bhītā yamunā yadunandanamuvāca cakitā vācaṃ patitā pādayornṛpa || 10 . 65 .
translation: O Soul of the universe so kind to his votaries! I have come to seek asylum in you, O glorious Lord. It behoves you to set me free (to flow in my own bed) as I did not know your supreme greatness.”

devanagari: राम राम महाबाहो न जाने तव विक्रमम् । यस्यैकांशेन विधृता जगती जगतः पते ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 028 ॥
english_devnagari: rāma rāma mahābāho na jāne tava vikramamyasyaikāṃśena vidhṛtā jagatī jagataḥ pate || 10 . 65 .
translation: Thus entreated, the mighty Balarāma then let go the Yamunā. And just like a king elephant entering water with his mates, he entered the waters (of the Yamunā) with the women (Gopīs).

devanagari: परं भावं भगवतो भगवन्मामजानतीम् । मोक्तुमर्हसि विश्वात्मन् प्रपन्नां भक्तवत्सल ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 029 ॥
english_devnagari: paraṃ bhāvaṃ bhagavato bhagavanmāmajānatīmmoktumarhasi viśvātman prapannāṃ bhaktavatsala || 10 . 65 .
translation: After having sported to his hearts content (in the water), Balarāma emerged from her waters when Kānti[5] (a form of goddess Lakṣmī) presented to him two clothes of nonwhite (blue) colour, highly precious ornaments and an auspicious (brilliant) garland.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.25.16 and states that Kānti is a synonym of Lakṣmī while VT. regards her as a form of Lakṣmī, the Spouse of the second Vyūha Saṅkarṣaṇa.

devanagari: ततो व्यमुञ्चद्यमुनां याचितो भगवान् बलः । विजगाह जलं स्त्रीभिः करेणुभिरिवेभराट् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 030 ॥
english_devnagari: tato vyamuñcadyamunāṃ yācito bhagavān balaḥvijagāha jalaṃ strībhiḥ kareṇubhirivebharāṭ || 10 . 65 .
translation: Putting on those blue garments, wearing the gold necklace, he shone like Airāvata, Indra’s elephant, when painted and tastefully ornamented.

devanagari: कामं विहृत्य सलिलादुत्तीर्णायासीताम्बरे । भूषणानि महार्हाणि ददौ कान्तिः शुभां स्रजम् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 031 ॥
english_devnagari: kāmaṃ vihṛtya salilāduttīrṇāyāsītāmbarebhūṣaṇāni mahārhāṇi dadau kāntiḥ śubhāṃ srajam || 10 . 65 .
translation: Even to this day, the Yamunā is seen to flow through the track (river bed) through which the Yamunā was dragged. It is an index of the great prowess of Balarāma of infinite prowess.

devanagari: वसित्वा वाससी नीले मालां आमुच्य काञ्चनीम् । रेये स्वलङ्कृतो लिप्तो माहेन्द्र इव वारणः ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 032 ॥
english_devnagari: vasitvā vāsasī nīle mālāṃ āmucya kāñcanīmreye svalaṅkṛto lipto māhendra iva vāraṇaḥ || 10 . 65 .
translation:
Balarāma’s mind was fascinated with the sweet qualities of the young women of Vraja, so he felt all nights passed in sports at Vraja as one continuous night.
[back to top]

devanagari: अद्यापि दृश्यते राजन् यमुनाकृष्टवर्त्मना । बलस्यानन्तवीर्यस्य वीर्यं सूचयतीव हि ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 033 ॥
english_devnagari: adyāpi dṛśyate rājan yamunākṛṣṭavartmanābalasyānantavīryasya vīryaṃ sūcayatīva hi || 10 . 65 .

devanagari: एवं सर्वा निशा याता एकेव रमतो व्रजे । रामस्याक्षिप्तचित्तस्य माधुर्यैर्व्रजयोषिताम् ॥ 10. 65. ॥ 034 ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sarvā niśā yātā ekeva ramato vrajerāmasyākṣiptacittasya mādhuryairvrajayoṣitām || 10 . 65 .
